meta.title: AWS-DEA-7: Streaming, Lake Formation, and Pipeline Operations

questions.stem: A streaming application needs custom stateful event processing logic over Kinesis records before writing results out. Which service is most appropriate in this scenario?

options.text: Amazon Managed Service for Apache Flink
options.explanation: `Amazon Managed Service for Apache Flink` best fits the scenario based on the official documentation. The other options either solve a different problem, require more custom work, or do not provide the capability asked for.

questions.reference: [What is Amazon Managed Service for Apache Flink?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/managed-flink/latest/java/what-is.html)

questions.stem: A team wants a petabyte-scale cloud data warehouse optimized for analytical queries on structured data. Which service should it use in this scenario?

options.explanation: `Amazon Redshift` best fits the scenario based on the official documentation. The other options either solve a different problem, require more custom work, or do not provide the capability asked for.

questions.stem: In Amazon Redshift, which design choice helps colocate rows that are frequently joined on the same key across large tables in this scenario?

options.explanation: `Distribution keys` best fits the scenario based on the official documentation. The other options either solve a different problem, require more custom work, or do not provide the capability asked for.

questions.stem: An architect wants Redshift table blocks stored in sorted order to improve range-restricted scans. Which table property is most relevant in this scenario?

options.explanation: `Sort key` best fits the scenario based on the official documentation. The other options either solve a different problem, require more custom work, or do not provide the capability asked for.

questions.stem: A team has infrequently accessed raw objects that must remain immediately retrievable at low cost. Which Amazon S3 storage class is a common fit in this scenario?

options.explanation: `S3 Standard-IA` best fits the scenario based on the official documentation. The other options either solve a different problem, require more custom work, or do not provide the capability asked for.

questions.stem: Which AWS service acts as the central technical metadata repository for table definitions used by Glue and Athena in this scenario?

options.explanation: `AWS Glue Data Catalog` best fits the scenario based on the official documentation. The other options either solve a different problem, require more custom work, or do not provide the capability asked for.

options.explanation: `Amazon Athena` best fits the scenario based on the official documentation. The other options either solve a different problem, require more custom work, or do not provide the capability asked for.

questions.stem: A central governance team wants to define fine-grained permissions for data lakes in Amazon S3 and manage table-level access consistently. Which service should anchor that design in this scenario?

options.explanation: `AWS Lake Formation` best fits the scenario based on the official documentation. The other options either solve a different problem, require more custom work, or do not provide the capability asked for.

options.explanation: `Lake Formation permissions` best fits the scenario based on the official documentation. The other options either solve a different problem, require more custom work, or do not provide the capability asked for.

questions.stem: A Glue job needs permission to read from Amazon S3 and write to the Data Catalog. What should be attached to the job role in this scenario?

options.explanation: `Athena workgroups` best fits the scenario based on the official documentation. The other options either solve a different problem, require more custom work, or do not provide the capability asked for.
